etymology.description: Áô∂(Áô∂) (bŇć, "two outstretched feet") + śģ≥(śģ≥) (shŇę, "hand with stick") = to beat or cut grass -> original form of śí•(śí•) (bŇć, "to shift")
etymology.interpretation: The character ÁôĻ combines the pictogram of two outstretched feet with the image of a hand holding a stick. This represents an action involved in striking or cutting grass, linking it to actions of sorting or moving, hence its relationship to the concept behind śí•(śí•) (bŇć, "to shift"). As a phono-semantic compound, Áô∂ provides the sound and the notion of spreading or stretching, while śģ≥ emphasizes the action-related meaning.
